title: Numbers - two kinds of division
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: Python has int and float. / always gives a float, // divides and floors, % gives the remainder.
explanation: 7 / 2 is 3.5 - regular division never throws information away. 7 // 2 is 3 - floor division keeps only the whole part. 7 % 2 is 1 - the remainder, which is how you check if a number is even. Three little operators, three different questions you can ask.
hints: The result value is 2.0, not 2. What does the .0 tell you?
/ does not look at whether the division is clean. It always answers the same way.
Even 8 / 4 gives 2.0 - regular division always returns a float.
